provenance.source_corpus: Restoration of Ecosystem Function by Soil Surface Inoculation with Biocrust in Mesic and Xeric Alpine Ecosystems

source_spans.text: Treatment plots receiving biocrust inoculation (B and BF) had significantly higher biocrust cover after 12 weeks (33% ± 3 and 32% ± 2 at Andesite, and 22% ± 2 and 20% ± 3 at Mt McIntyre; Table 1, Figure 3).

Treatment plots receiving biocrust inoculation (B and BF) had significantly higher biocrust cover after 12 weeks (33% ± 3 and 32% ± 2 at Andesite, and 22% ± 2 and 20% ± 3 at Mt McIntyre; Table 1, Figure 3).
Biocrust cover and soil surface nitrogen fixation were enhanced by inoculation after 12 weeks at the mesic site.

at Mt McIntyre, 12 weeks following inoculation, the fertilizer treatment (F) had significantly lower EPS (Figure 5, 76 ± 19 µg of glucose per g of biocrust) compared to all other treatments (Figure 5, B = 250 ± 92, BF = 217 ± 49, C = 170 ± 42 µg of glucose per g of biocrust, respectively).

at Mt McIntyre, 12 weeks following inoculation, the fertilizer treatment (F) had significantly lower EPS (Figure 5, 76 ± 19 µg of glucose per g of biocrust) compared to all other treatments (Figure 5, B = 250 ± 92, BF = 217 ± 49, C = 170 ± 42 µg of glucose per g of biocrust, respectively).
At Andesite, DOC was significantly higher in inoculated plots (B = 75 ± 4.9 and BF = 122 ± 14 ppm) than in uninoculated plots (C = 9.2 ± 1.3 and F = 45 ± 5.8 ppm).
